Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log of 15 events from a machine M1 We need to group events into phases activities and assign labels Lets analyze the sensor readings over time

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 1 to 3 Low temperature 20 to 21 low pressure 1 bar zero vibration zero material flow tool position 0 low energy 04 to 05 This appears to be an idle or standby state

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 4 Temperature jumps to 50 vibration to 30 material flow 5 units per s tool position moves to 5 mm energy increases to 20 So something starts

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 4 to 6 Temperature rising 50 to 55 to 58 pressure increases slightly 1 to 15 bar vibration rising 30 to 35 to 38 material flow steady at 5 tool position increasing 5 to 7 to 10 energy rising 2 to 25 to 3 This seems like an active process with increasing activity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 7 to 8 Temperature drops to 40 pressure 2 bar vibration drops to 10 material flow drops to 2 tool position at 15 mm energy 2 kWh This is a different phase moderate temperature higher pressure than before lower vibration lower flow tool position steady

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 9 Temperature spikes to 80 vibration jumps to 50 material flow 0 tool position still 15 energy jumps to 5 kWh This is a high energy high temperature high vibration event with no material flow Possibly a welding or heating operation without material feeding

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 10 Temperature drops to 30 pressure back to 1 bar vibration 1 material flow 0 tool position 15 energy 1 kWh This could be cooling or a pause

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 11 to 12 Temperature 25 pressure 1 vibration 5 material flow 1 tool position 20 energy around 15 to 16 This seems like a light activity with some material flow and tool movement

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Event 13 to 15 Similar to events 1 to 3 low temperature pressure 1 vibration 0 flow 0 tool position 0 low energy Back to idle

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Looking at the whole sequence it seems like a cycle idle to some process to another process to a high energy process to cooling light activity to idle

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cutting Metal Often involves high vibration maybe temperature increase material flow if it is a cutting fluid or material being fed tool movement Could correspond to events 4 to 6 where we see increasing temperature vibration and tool position with material flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Assembling Parts Typically lower energy maybe some pressure or tool movement but not high temperature or vibration Could be events 7 to 8 moderate temperature steady tool position some pressure lower vibration and flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Welding High temperature possibly high energy might have no material flow tool position steady holding the weld Event 9 looks like welding very high temperature 80 high vibration 50 high energy 5 kWh no material flow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Quality Inspection Might involve sensors low energy little movement But here we have events 11 to 12 with some tool movement flow and vibration Could be a finishing or inspection step Or maybe packaging

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively events 10 to 12 might be cooling and then packaging or assembly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Given the typical steps we might label 1 Idle Ready Events 1 to 3 and 13 to 15 2 Cutting Machining Events 4 to 6 3 Assembly Fastening Events 7 to 8 4 Welding Joining Event 9 5 Cooling Pause Event 10 6 Inspection Finishing Events 11 to 12

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we could group events 10 to 12 as a single phase Cooling and Finishing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The machine starts idle then begins cutting machining events 4 to 6 then transitions to a pressure based operation events 7 to 8 then welding event 9 then cools down event 10 then does some light operation events 11 to 12 then returns to idle

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to assign intuitive labels Consider typical manufacturing cutting then assembling which might involve pressing or fastening then welding then inspection then back to idle

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The tool position starts at 0 moves during cutting 5 7 10 then jumps to 15 and stays there during assembling and welding then moves to 20 during inspection then back to 0 at idle That makes sense

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Material flow during cutting is high 5 during assembling lower 2 during welding 0 during inspection 1 Energy cutting medium 2 to 3 assembling medium 2 welding high 5 inspection low 15

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Maybe the process is Cutting then Assembling then Welding then Cooling then Inspection then Idle That seems plausible
